focus: V10 Hard-gate realism timing + cache math

findings.summary: The cache-hit-rate hard gate in 005 is mathematically impossible as written. A synthetic 30-turn replay defined as 20 unique prompts plus 10 repeats yields at most 10 cache hits out of 30 total turns (33.3%), so it cannot satisfy a >=60% overall hit-rate gate under 004's exact-prompt cache policy.

005's timing-harness runtime budget is internally inconsistent. The spec says the harness records 50 samples across 4 lanes, but its <2 minute NFR is below the lower bound implied by its own 1500 ms cold, 1100 ms warm, and signature-changed miss lanes; the minimum run time is roughly 187.5 seconds before setup overhead.

The 50 ms latency gate itself is realistic if it remains explicitly cache-hit-only. 004's producer-only warm-hit budget is <=10 ms, while 006 and 007 already reserve total hook p95 <=60 ms on cache hit, leaving parse/serialize/adapter headroom without conflicting with 005's cold/warm subprocess lanes.
